Notice Of Motion To Shorten The Time Within Which Deft Must Respond To Pltf'S Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Jun-13-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Brett Gibbs, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and moving party: Randall G. Tipton
Kristian D. Whitten, Esq., appeared for defendant and opposing party: Franchise Tax Board

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Randall G. Tipton's Motion To Shorten Time Within Which Defendant Must Respond To Plaintiff's Discovery is denied. The court finds that the scope of discovery is limited, and there is no good cause to order that Defendant respond to the discovery propounded on shortened time. On this motion the court does not decide whether any discovery is allowed in this action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
